display.purpose: Page-level titles in settings and onboarding heroes. Rare.

headline.purpose: Section headers in dialogs and major panels.

title.purpose: Card titles, dialog titles, panel headers.

body.purpose: Default text; inspector field labels, dialog body, descriptions. 65-75ch for prose.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrow labels above grouped controls; small role markers.

mono.purpose: Code blocks, node IDs, numeric table cells, terminal output.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Tooltips and small popovers lifting off panels.

shadows.name: editor-menu
shadows.purpose: Node context menus and command menus overlaying the canvas.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Hover micro-interactions, icon state changes, transforms.

motion.name: normal
motion.purpose: Standard UI transitions: color, border, opacity, shadow.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default primary action in chrome. Studio Blue at rest, magenta-tinted on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #6690d4; color: #FCFCFC; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.005em; padding: 6px 14px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #E35BFF; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E879F9; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Quiet action used for the bulk of chrome buttons. Transparent at rest, tonal lift on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #F7F8F8; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; padding: 6px 14px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #1B1D21; border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.16); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6690d4; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Pill-shaped tag. Used for model labels, status badges, filter pills.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #1B1D21; color: #8A8F98; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Provider Pill (Local)
components.description: Provider badge variant. Tints background with provider hue at low opacity.
components.html: Local
components.css: .ds-pill-local { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: rgba(134,239,172,0.10); color: #86EFAC; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; padding: 2px 8px 2px 6px; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Inspector / form text field. Hairline border, primary focus, no outer glow.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #101113; color: #F7F8F8; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 15px; padding: 6px 8px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 4px; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8A8F98; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.16); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #6690d4; }

components.name: Node Card
components.description: ReactFlow node body. Header strip in a darker tint, body in node surface, 8px radius.
components.html:
Image Generator
prompta city at dusk
steps28
components.css: .ds-node { background: #1B1D21; color: #F7F8F8; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; width: 220px; box-shadow: none; transition: outline-color 150ms ease; outline: 1px solid transparent; } .ds-node:hover { outline-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); } .ds-node-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: #141518; padding: 4px 8px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8A8F98; height: 28px; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-node-body { padding: 6px 8px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-node-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; font-size: 13px; } .ds-node-label { color: #8A8F98; } .ds-node-value { color: #F7F8F8; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Floating tooltip with blur backdrop, hairline highlight, soft drop.
components.css: .ds-tooltip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: rgba(12,13,16,0.96); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); color: #F7F8F8; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 6px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 6px; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5), 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.04); } .ds-kbd { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); padding: 1px 4px; border-radius: 3px; }

narrative.overview: NodeTool is a dark studio lit by what you're making. The room recedes; the artifacts glow. The canvas is the floor of the studio; panels and toolbars are quiet metal in slightly lifted tones; the only saturated color in a resting workflow is the node-teal of the running graph and the magenta of the user's current selection. The system is dense without being cramped: Inter at 15px body with a tight 1.125 scale ratio means a node inspector can show twenty fields without feeling like a wall. The eight-step radius scale reserves the larger radii for dialogs and assets, keeping working surfaces at 4-8px so the canvas reads as precision instrumentation, not a card grid.
keyCharacteristics: Dark by default; the canvas is the floor, panels are lifted tonal layers.
Two saturated accents only (Studio Blue, Stage Magenta); canvas-teal belongs to nodes, not chrome.
Inter for everything UI; JetBrains Mono for code, IDs, and numeric data.
Tight rounded scale; 4-8px on working surfaces, 12-20px reserved for dialogs and asset cards.
Tonal layering for depth; shadows reserved for floating chrome.

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: Chrome uses Studio Blue and Stage Magenta only. Node Teal, Input Slate, Output Plum, and the semantic colors belong to the canvas and the running workflow.

rules.name: The Canvas-Wins Rule
rules.body: When a generated artifact renders, the surrounding chrome desaturates: panels stay neutral, accents pause. The artifact is the only saturated thing on screen.

rules.name: The Tinted-Near-Black Rule
rules.body: Never #000. Surface neutrals are tinted toward warm-cool gray. Pure black flattens the tonal hierarchy.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Inter for chrome; JetBrains Mono for content that is itself code or numeric. Never mix a third family.

rules.name: The Fixed-rem Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are fixed (rem/em), not fluid. A node inspector at 15px reads the same in a 320px-wide panel as in a 600px-wide one.

rules.name: The Tight-Tracking Rule
rules.body: Headings tighten tracking (-0.01em to -0.02em); body stays neutral; uppercase labels open up to 0.04em.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Panels, cards, nodes, and inputs cast no shadow at rest. If a surface is gaining shadow on hover, it is wrong; use a tonal change instead.

rules.name: The Lift-When-Floating Rule
rules.body: Anything that visually leaves the page (tooltip, popover, menu, dialog) gets shadow. The shadow's job is 'I am not part of the page beneath me', not 'I am important'.

dos: Do use the four-tier tonal ladder (#0A0B0D → #101113 → #1B1D21 → #22252A) for depth. Lifting via tone, not shadow.
Do reserve primary blue and attention magenta for chrome actions and emphasis only.
Do keep node working surfaces at rounded.node (8px); buttons at 4-6px; dialogs at 20px. The radius scale is meaningful.
Do use ui_primitives/ components for every chrome element. Raw MUI imports outside ui_primitives/ and editor_ui/ are out of spec.
Do pair every semantic color (error/warning/success/info) with an icon or shape; color is never the only signal.
Do use JetBrains Mono for code, IDs, timestamps, and numeric table cells; everything else is Inter.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion. Standard transitions are 120-200ms; panel/drawer is 350ms.
Do allow density. Inspectors, node libraries, and asset grids can carry a lot; earn it with rhythm of varied spacing and the type scale.
donts: Don't ship a generic SaaS dashboard: no hero-metric tiles, no cream-card grids, no gradient CTAs, no identical card-grids of 'features'.
Don't drift into n8n / Zapier / enterprise no-code styling.
Don't drift into ComfyUI developer-raw (unstyled controls, debug-color overlays, no hierarchy).
Don't drift into Notion / Apple minimalism (sterile whitespace, thin grays, no personality).
Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. All neutrals are tinted.
Don't use a side-stripe border (border-left > 1px) on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Solid colors; emphasis via weight or size.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. The one allowed blur is the tooltip surface.
Don't use box-shadow on resting surfaces. Shadow appears only on floating chrome.
Don't use a display or serif font in UI labels, buttons, or data.
Don't use fluid type (clamp) in product chrome; fixed rem only.
Don't use em dashes in UI copy. Commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't reach for a modal as the first thought. Exhaust inline and progressive disclosure first.
Don't wrap everything in a card. Nested cards are always wrong.
